import os
import psycopg2

conn = psycopg2.connect(os.environ[''DATABASE_URL''])
cur = conn.cursor()

brand_id = 15

# Check responses for brand 15
cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*), batch_id FROM responses WHERE brand_id = %s GROUP BY batch_id'', (brand_id,))
resp_counts = cur.fetchall()
print(''Response counts by batch:'', resp_counts)

# Check if responses are analyzed
cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*), analyzed_at IS NOT NULL as is_analyzed FROM responses WHERE brand_id = %s GROUP BY analyzed_at IS NOT NULL'', (brand_id,))
analyzed_counts = cur.fetchall()
print(''Analyzed counts:'', analyzed_counts)

# Sample response
cur.execute(''SELECT id, batch_id, platform, brand_mentioned, sentiment, analyzed_at FROM responses WHERE brand_id = %s LIMIT 5'', (brand_id,))
samples = cur.fetchall()
print(''Sample responses:'', samples)

conn.close()
")

import os
import psycopg2

conn = psycopg2.connect(os.environ[''DATABASE_URL''])
cur = conn.cursor()

# Check if queries, competitors, descriptors exist for brand 15
cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM queries WHERE brand_id = 15 AND user_id = 10'')
q_count = cur.fetchone()[0]
print(f''Queries for brand 15: {q_count}'')

cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM competitors WHERE brand_id = 15 AND user_id = 10'')
c_count = cur.fetchone()[0]
print(f''Competitors for brand 15: {c_count}'')

cur.execute(''SELECT COUNT(*) FROM target_descriptors WHERE brand_id = 15 AND user_id = 10'')
d_count = cur.fetchone()[0]
print(f''Target descriptors for brand 15: {d_count}'')

conn.close()
")
